label: Tiles
title: Tile API
lead: Register terrain tiles with stable block indices, vanilla block behavior, and normal world or Tilemap placement.
bodyHtml:
Register a terrain tile

TileRegistry.Register adds a Unity Tile to every WorldGeneration.tiles palette, supplies the matching vanilla BlockInfo, and can optionally hook the tile into vanilla-style ore generation.

TileRegistry.Register(36, new CustomTileDefinition
{
    ID = "galena",
    Name = "Galena",
    Sprite = AssetLoader.LoadEmbeddedSprite("Images.galena.png", 8f),
    Health = 300f,
    HitSound = "stone",
    StepSound = "Gravel",
    SleepQuality = Body.SleepQuality.Mediocre,
    SpawnAmount = 0.5f,
    SpawnLayers = TileRegistry.AllLayersExcept(1, 3),
    GenerationStyle = TileGenerationStyle.Vein | TileGenerationStyle.Outskirt
});

Tile indices are written into the world's block array and saves. Pick an unused index of 36 or higher, keep it assigned to the same tile forever, and coordinate indices with other mods you expect players to combine. CUCoreLib rejects vanilla indices and duplicate custom registrations instead of changing an existing block's meaning.

Place the registered tile

Registration makes the tile available; your mod still decides where it appears. For live world terrain, use TileRegistry.SetBlock or vanilla WorldGeneration.SetBlock with the registered index.

Vector2Int blockPosition = WorldGeneration.world.WorldToBlockPos(transform.position);
TileRegistry.SetBlock(WorldGeneration.world, blockPosition, 36);

// The vanilla equivalent after registration:
WorldGeneration.world.SetBlock(blockPosition, 36);

Tilemap.SetTile() is one of the ways of adding the tile into the game. Use TileRegistry.TryGetTile to retrieve the registered TileBase, then place it into a structure or another Unity Tilemap. When the game imports that tilemap through its normal structure generation methods, it resolves the tile back to the registered block index.

For now, you'll have to swap into Unity for this. (Eventually I'll have the Custom Structures webapp support it though ^^)

if (TileRegistry.TryGetTile(36, out TileBase galenaTile))
{
    structureTilemap.SetTile(new Vector3Int(4, -2, 0), galenaTile);
}

Use SetBlockNoUpdate() only while batching generation work where the caller will refresh affected chunks afterward.

CustomTileDefinition fields

FieldTypePurpose
IDstringStable locale key and default Unity tile name.
NamestringRegistered as other.ID.
SpriteSpriteRequired tile artwork, usually loaded through AssetLoader.
TileNamestringOptional Unity object name. Defaults to ID.
ColorColorSprite tint. Defaults to white.
ColliderTypeTile.ColliderTypeUnity Tilemap collider behavior. Defaults to Grid.
HealthfloatDamage required to break the block.
HitSoundstringVanilla hit-sound shorthand. You can point at another tile or a vanilla sound id.
HitSoundClipAudioClipDirect hit sound override. Takes priority over HitSound.
StepSoundstringVanilla sound id used for footsteps.
SleepQualityBody.SleepQualityRest quality while lying on the tile.
NoVariationboolDisables the game's visual tile variation. (random flips)
MetallicboolEnables vanilla metallic damage behavior.
ToxicityfloatUses the vanilla block toxicity (irradiation) field.
SlipperyboolUses the vanilla slippery-floor behavior.
SpawnAmountfloatOptional copper-spawn multiplier. 0f disables automatic spawning, 1f matches copper, 2f doubles it, and 0.5f halves it.
SpawnLayersintBitmask for allowed in-game layers. Defaults to all layers. Use helpers like TileRegistry.LayersToMask(2, 4, 5) or TileRegistry.AllLayersExcept(1, 3).
GenerationStyleTileGenerationStyleByte-backed preset flags for automatic worldgen shape. Defaults to Vein. Combine flags like Vein | Outskirt when you want mixed behavior.
DropsItemDrop[]Optional break drops using the same id, chance, conditionMin, and conditionMax fields as custom building drops.
CustomDataDictionary<string, object>Registration-time metadata for your own tile behavior, such as a custom leadToxicity value read later with TileRegistry.TryGetCustomData<T>.

Automatic ore-style spawning

Set SpawnAmount above 0f and CUCoreLib will distribute the tile during WorldGeneration.GenerateOres() using the same vein-walk pattern vanilla copper uses. The value is a multiplier on copper's own spawn count, and the current run's oreamount setting still scales the density.

SpawnLayers uses 1-based layer numbers for mod authors. Layer 1 means the first playable layer, layer 4 means biome depth 3, and so on. Leave it alone for all layers, or use the helper masks when you want exclusions.

GenerationStyle is a byte flag enum with these presets: Vein, HeavyVeins, Singular, Stripe, Inner, and Outskirt. If you combine more than one, CUCoreLib splits the spawn budget across the selected presets so SpawnAmount stays predictable.

TileRegistry.Register(37, new CustomTileDefinition
{
    ID = "auric",
    Name = "Auric",
    Sprite = AssetLoader.LoadEmbeddedSprite("Images.auric.png", 8f),
    Health = 777f,
    HitSound = "crystal",
    StepSound = "Rock",
    Metallic = true,
    SpawnAmount = 2f,
    SpawnLayers = TileRegistry.LayersToMask(4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10),
    GenerationStyle = TileGenerationStyle.HeavyVeins | TileGenerationStyle.Inner
});
